featureName: Integration of Existing Stealth Scraper and HTML Parser
description: Integrate and adapt the existing stealth_page_fetch.py script for use within the Job Ingestion Node. Develop a targeted HTML parser based on parsed_data_map.md to extract key job details from the scraped content before LLM processing.

userStories.title: Integrate Existing Stealth Scraper Utility
userStories.description: As a system coordinator, I want to integrate the existing `stealth_page_fetch.py` script into the project so that it can be called by the Job Ingestion Node to retrieve and clean job page content.
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario: Successfully fetching and cleaning HTML using the existing script
When the integrated scraper utility is called from the Job Ingestion Node
Then it executes the `stealth_page_fetch.py` logic, returning cleaned markdown content
Given the underlying script fails (e.g., chromedriver issue, timeout)
When the scraper utility is called
Then it catches the exception and raises a standardized `ScraperError` that the ingestion node can handle

tasks.description: Move the logic from `stealth_page_fetch.py` into a reusable function or class within a new `src/utils/scraper.py` module.
Add `undetected-chromedriver` and its dependencies (`beautifulsoup4`, `markdownify`) to the `pyproject.toml` file.
Implement robust error handling within the scraper utility to wrap potential exceptions in a custom `ScraperError`.
Write integration tests for the scraper utility, mocking the webdriver to test success and failure cases without actual web requests.

userStories.notes: The scraper will be a more complex dependency than a simple HTTP client, so error handling is critical.

userStories.title: Implement Targeted Data Extraction Using HTML Mapping
userStories.description: As a system coordinator, I want a utility that uses the `parsed_data_map.md` guidelines to extract key data sections from the raw HTML, so that a cleaner, more focused context can be sent to the LLM for final parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: Scenario: Extracting key job sections from HTML
Given raw HTML content from a job page
When the HTML parsing utility is called
Then it uses BeautifulSoup and the selectors from `parsed_data_map.md` to extract the Job Title, Company Info, Core Description, and Job Criteria
And it returns a simplified, concatenated text block containing only these essential sections
Scenario: Handling HTML with missing sections
Given raw HTML that does not contain one of the mapped sections (e.g., no salary info)
When the HTML parsing utility is called
Then it gracefully skips the missing section and returns the content it did find without errors

tasks.description: Implement an `extract_job_details` function that takes raw HTML and returns a string containing only the text from the targeted selectors.
Integrate the `html_parser` into the `JobIngestionNode` to be called after the scraper runs but before the LLM is invoked.
Write unit tests for the parser using sample HTML files to verify that it correctly extracts data and handles missing elements.

userStories.notes: This pre-processing step should reduce noise and improve the reliability of the final LLM-based parsing into the JobDetailsSchema.
